subject: Biology
title: The Discovery of Microbes
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Discovery of Microbes usable.

hook.text: In 1676, Leeuwenhoek peered through a homemade lens and saw creatures no human had ever seen.

buildup.text: He found them in rainwater, teeth scrapings, and pond water—tiny beings everywhere.

discovery.text: He called them 'animalcules' and wrote detailed letters to the Royal Society describing them.

twist.text: Nobody believed him at first. The Royal Society sent a delegation to verify his claims.

climax.text: His observations opened an entirely new world. Microbiology was born from a draper's hobby.

punchline.text: A cloth merchant discovered a hidden world.

quiz.question: What was Leeuwenhoek's profession before discovering microbes?
